data.title: MP Materials hits record high after smaller loss, Pentagon and Apple boost
data.description:
MP Materials shares hit record high on Friday, after the U.S. rare earths miner posted higher output, a smaller-than-expected quarterly loss and forecast further production gains.

Shares of the company jumped 10.45% to $78.50 in early trading. Las Vegas-based MP operates the only U.S. rare earths mine and has seen its stock more than quadruple this year as Washington moves to secure domestic supplies and cut dependence on China.

Production of neodymium and praseodymium (NdPr), the most in-demand rare earths, jumped nearly 120% in the second quarter to a record 597 metric tons.

In the current quarter, NdPr oxide production is expected to rise 10% to 20% sequentially, Founder and Chief Operating Officer Michael Rosenthal said on a post-earnings call.

Analysts at TD Cowen said strong upstream volumes and magnetics output drove a 40% EBITDA beat, with commercial magnet production on track by the year-end.

They now expect the company to hit a 60,000-tonne annual run-rate of rare earth oxide output a year ahead of schedule.

The results come weeks after MP signed a multibillion-dollar deal with the U.S. Department of Defense that guarantees a $110 per kg price floor for NdPr, nearly double the Chinese market levels, and could make the Pentagon its biggest shareholder.

That was followed by a $500 million rare earth magnet supply agreement with Apple , whose prepayments will cover most of the cost to expand MP's Independence mine in Texas.

Stage III magnetics operations are already EBITDA-positive and, alongside government and Apple funding, leave the company well-capitalized to accelerate growth, brokerage DA Davidson said.

MP posted an adjusted loss of 13 cents per share for second quarter, while analysts estimated a 19-cent loss.

data.title: Staar Surgical's Top Holder Questions Deal — Market Talk
data.description: Staar Surgical's largest shareholder is raising questions about the implantable-lens maker's $1.5 billion deal to be acquired by Swiss eye-care giant Alcon. New York private investment firm Broadwood Capital, which owns more than 27% of Staar, says in an SEC filing that it hasn't decided whether it will support the buyout and that it wants to see documents relating to the process that led to the deal. Broadwood says it also plans to explore alternatives to the deal, including through contact with possible other buyers. Staar up 1% to $27.21, shy of the $28/share takeover price. (colin.kellaher@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0940 ET - Staar Surgical's largest shareholder is raising questions about the implantable-lens maker's $1.5 billion deal to be acquired by Swiss eye-care giant Alcon. New York private investment firm Broadwood Capital, which owns more than 27% of Staar, says in an SEC filing that it hasn't decided whether it will support the buyout and that it wants to see documents relating to the process that led to the deal. Broadwood says it also plans to explore alternatives to the deal, including through contact with possible other buyers. Staar up 1% to $27.21, shy of the $28/share takeover price. (colin.kellaher@wsj.com)

0927 ET - Europe's chipmakers and drugmakers look secure, for now, from a more damaging set of U.S. trade tariffs, Capital Economics' Jack Allen-Reynolds writes in a note to clients. The European Commission says pharmaceutical products and semiconductors are included in the 15% tariffs the EU executive agreed with Washington at the end of last month. The sectors shouldn't therefore be subject to higher duties on their imports to the U.S., Allen-Reynolds says. Pharma and chip companies could even see some positive effect from President Trump's tariff policy in case higher levies are imposed on other countries, he says. "European firms would [then] become more competitive relative to other non-U.S. producers." (joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby)

0919 ET - Sweetgreen's softer results will likely continue in the near-term as traffic gets weighed down by industry-wide pressures and consumers' increasing focus on value, KeyBanc Capital Markets analysts say in a note. Sweetgreen could see some stabilizing or improving trends as brand-specific headwinds, like the transition of its loyalty program, begin to subside, and as tactics like portion sizing and better operational execution start to bear fruit, the analysts note. But overall, the company is set up for only modest sequential improvement in the rest of the year as near-term uncertainty continues, they say. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

0902 ET - "Trade Desk's large-brand exposure has historically been the hull of its ship," Benchmark analysts say in a research note. "However, near-term tariff waves present a perhaps bumpy ride near-term." The company, which helps advertisers buy and optimize digital ad campaigns, didn't quantify the impact of a slowdown among large-brand advertising in 2Q, but the analysts estimate it at a low double-digit revenue impact, based on the 2-point sequential drop in gross domestic billings. "While much of our ad tech coverage has largely shrugged-off tariff-related pressures, most of these companies are much more exposed to small- and midsize-business customers," the analysts write. Trade Desk shares tumble 33% premarket. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

0852 ET - Custom duties are bad news for Swiss watchmakers, but the U.S. will continue to be a key market, Yves Bugmann, president of the Federation of the Swiss Watch Industry says. President Trump imposed duties of 39% on Swiss goods imported to the U.S. Despite the implications of the levies, Bugmann remains confident in the future and the potential of the U.S. market for the industry. Many brands were able to ship products to the U.S in the recent months, before the implementation of tariffs, he says, adding that this provides a "valuable degree of flexibility" for the time being. "What matters now is that the Swiss government gives priority to seeking a better solution that serves the interests of both sides," Bugmann adds. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

0850 ET - Trade Desk's 2Q results were largely in line with Wall Street's expectations, Oppenheimer analysts say in a research note. But the results didn't live up to the expectations set by strong recent results from competitors such as Meta, Google and Amazon. Trade Desk's outlook reflects a multi-quarter slowdown, which management blamed on tariff uncertainty, a slower pace of next-generation ad-tech adoption by some large advertisers and lack of excess advertising budgets amid macro uncertainty. The analysts note that, while it's clear Trade Desk isn't executing at peak levels, the company's revised outlook is likely already priced in. Oppenheimer maintains its outperform rating but lowers its price target to $70 from $110. Trade Desk shares drop 33% to $59.09 premarket. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

0845 ET - The U.S.'s tariffs on Swiss goods pose a threat to the tech sector, a Swiss industry group says. The Trump administration imposed 39% levies on Swiss goods imported into the U.S. "If this horrendous tariff burden remains in place, it will mean the de facto death of the export business of the Swiss tech industry to the U.S.," Swissmem says. Swissmem is an association for Switzerland's small- and medium-sized, as well as major technology firms. Competitors in the EU and Japan face lower tariff rates, the association adds. "Switzerland must not accept this situation and should continue to negotiate." (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

0825 ET - The Trade Desk reported healthy 2Q results and in-line 3Q guidance, but management commentary amplified investors' worries about rising competitive intensity, the company's relationship with key agency partners and the ultimate size of the long-term market opportunity, Wedbush analysts say in a research note. "The narrative has turned cautious, and we highlight multiple reports in recent months suggesting Amazon has more aggressively improved its demand-side platform capabilities and offerings, with several marketers migrating larger budgets from Trade Desk to Amazon's DSP," the analysts write. Amazon's demand-side platform directly competes with Trade Desk, helping advertisers buy and optimize digital ad campaigns. Trade Desk shares fall 32% in premarket trading. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

0726 ET - Novartis's midterm prospects look increasingly well supported, with growth now less reliant on immunology drug Cosentyx that is due to lose patent protection in 2029, analysts at Morgan Stanley say in a research note. The Swiss pharma company is targeting sales growth of 5% over the 2024-2029 period and Morgan Stanley says its own forecasts are closing the gap to that guidance. "Although [Novartis's second-quarter] results did not deliver the sales guidance raise hoped for by investors, the quality of the midterm growth over 2024-29 has improved in our view," the analysts say. The bank raised its recommendation on the stock to equalweight from underweight, while lifting its target price to 100 Swiss francs from 91 francs. Shares rise 0.5% to 93.52 francs. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0718 ET - Germany will likely have to revise down the already weak second-quarter GDP contraction of 0.1% due to industrial data that showed production slumped 1.9% on month in June, Berenberg's Holger Schmieding says. Whereas data had so far indicated that the slippage in 2Q was no more than a minor setback after two quarters of growth, new figures show a more pronounced reversal of fortunes, the analyst says in a note. However, slightly more upbeat surveys for July prompt a small lift to Berenberg's 3Q GDP forecasts from flat to 0.1%. Retail sales grew for a fifth quarter in a row in 2Q, suggesting German consumers are opening their wallets more, and companies also seem to be gearing up to invest more, he adds. (edward.frankl@wsj.com)

0707 ET - Swiss pharma giant Novartis has the balance-sheet and cash-flow strength to accelerate dealmaking and mitigate an upcoming wave of patent expirations for its drugs from 2029, Morgan Stanley analysts say in a research note. "Novartis has one of the strongest balance sheets in European large pharma," Morgan Stanley says. Analysts at the bank estimate Novartis would have $55 billion of merger-and-acquisition firepower if it increased its debt ratio. The company should be able to grow through the end of patent protection for its immunology drug Cosentyx in 2029, but will need pipeline drugs to be successful and dealmaking to offset the hits from other medicines losing exclusivity beyond that, according to Morgan Stanley. Shares rise 0.4%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0703 ET - The potential application of U.S. tariffs on imports of one-kilo gold bars would have a profound impact on the global bullion market, says Commerzbank Research's Carsten Fritsch. According to a Financial Times report, the U.S. Customs Border Protection agency said in a ruling letter that gold bars weighing one kilogram and 100 ounces should be classified under a code subject to levies. "If this is not a mistaken classification, it would have serious implications for the gold market," the commodity analyst says. "It would mean that gold bars of this size imported from Switzerland into the U.S. would be subject to a 39% tariff." In European afternoon trade, gold futures trade 0.6% higher at $3,475.40 a troy ounce after reaching a peak of $3,534.20 earlier in the session. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

data.title: Dow Opens Higher; Gold Prices Hit Record High — WSJ
data.description:
By WSJ Staff

U.S stocks are on track for gains at the end of a week heavy on tariff updates, but light on market turbulence.

Stocks edged higher early Friday. The Dow industrials are currently up more than 1% for the week. The Nasdaq Composite, which hit a record high Thursday, has gained more than 3%.

Most markets posted modest moves early in the day, but bullion proved an exception. Gold futures hit an intraday record high after the Financial Times said the U.S. had imposed levies on one-kilo bars. Such a move would be a fresh tariff blow for Switzerland, a major refining hub.

Gold prices have risen for five of the past six days, boosted in part by hopes the Federal Reserve will soon cut interest rates.

Late Thursday, President Trump chose economic adviser Stephen Miran to fill a vacancy on the Federal Reserve's board of governors until January. Miran will replace Adriana Kugler, who is expected to depart Friday.

More earnings rolled in after hours on Thursday, including from Motorola, Square owner Block and Live Nation, the largest concert promoter.

In recent trading:

Stocks crept higher, with all three major indexes in the green.

Intel shares wavered after Thursday's steep loss. The chipmaker said CEO Lip-Bu Tan had the board's support, after Trump called for his resignation.

Treasury prices inched lower, pushing up yields. The 10-year yield rose to around 4.25%.

The dollar strengthened against the euro, the Swiss franc and the yen.

Bitcoin prices held above $116,000 after Trump signed an executive order making it easier for Americans to invest retirement savings in assets such as cryptocurrency.

Gold futures topped $3,500 per troy ounce, before paring back.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Boralex Inc reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Boralex Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 29 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 53 cents. The mean expectation of five analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 49 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 39 cents to 19 cents per share.

  • Revenue fell 12.7% to C$185.00 million from a year ago; analysts expected C$267.97 million.

  • Boralex Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was 29 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of C$30 million.

  • Boralex Inc shares had fallen by 3.3% this quarter and gained 6.6% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 218.3% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, four analysts negatively revised earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 7 "strong buy" or "buy," 3 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the electric utilities peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Boralex Inc is C$38.50, about 20.5% above its last closing price of C$30.61

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data August 8 at 01:40 p.m. UTC. ​All figures in Canadian dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Intrusion Inc expected to post a loss of 9 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Intrusion Inc is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on August 12 for the period ending June 30 2025

  • The Plano Texas-based company is expected to report a 23.1% increase in revenue to $1.798 million from $1.46 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 2 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Intrusion Inc is for a loss of 9 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 1 "strong buy" or "buy," 1 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Intrusion Inc is $6.75, about 76.3% above its last closing price of $1.60

data.title: S&P 500 Rises 20.44 Points (0.32%)
Nasdaq Composite Rises 62.85 Points (0.30%)
Meta, Robby Starbuck Settle AI Defamation Lawsuit — WSJ
data.description:
By Joseph De Avila

Meta Platforms has settled a defamation lawsuit with conservative activist Robby Starbuck, who alleged the company's artificial intelligence chatbot falsely asserted he participated in the Jan. 6, 2021, riot at the U.S. Capitol.

Under the settlement, Starbuck will advise Meta, the owner of Facebook, on efforts to curb what they describe as political bias in its AI tools.

"Since engaging on these important issues with Robby, Meta has made tremendous strides to improve the accuracy of Meta AI and mitigate ideological and political bias," Meta and Starbuck said in a statement.

Additional terms of the settlement weren't disclosed. Starbuck originally sought more than $5 million in damages for the lawsuit he filed in Delaware Superior Court in April.

During a CNBC interview Friday, Starbuck declined to say whether he had been paid by Meta as part of the settlement.

He said he had talked to Meta executives and engineers after the suit was filed about AI training and what needed to be done to address the issue.

The activist said his goal was ethical AI that remained neutral on political issues.

"We don't want a future where you've got AI putting the thumb on the scale when it comes to politics," he said.

Starbuck has gained notoriety for his efforts to pressure large companies to abandon their diversity, equity and inclusion policies.

He said he found out about the AI comments last summer when he was pushing for Harley-Davidson to change its DEI policies. A Harley dealer in Vermont posted a screenshot on X purportedly showing an Meta AI response saying Starbuck was at the Capitol riot and that he was linked to QAnon.

Starbuck denied the allegations and said in a social-media post that "Meta will hear from my lawyers." He says Meta AI was still making the same unproven claims about him months later.

He took to social media to ask Meta executives, including Chief Executive Mark Zuckerberg, to take down the inaccurate information. Starbuck said his lawyer sent Meta a cease-and-desist letter.

Meta has been working on removing political bias in its AI tools. AI systems have historically leaned left on contentious political and social topics based on how they have been trained, Meta said in a blog post in April.

"Our goal is to remove bias from our AI models and to make sure that Llama can understand and articulate both sides of a contentious issue," Meta said in the post, referring to its AI model.

Write to Joseph De Avila at joseph.deavila@wsj.com

data.title: DJIA Rises 165.51 Points (0.38%)
COASTAL FINANCIAL CORP SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Coastal Financial Corporation, a prominent financial institution operating primarily in the Puget Sound region, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's robust financial performance and strategic operational initiatives, underscoring its commitment to growth and shareholder value.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Interest Income: $107.8 million, reflecting a growth driven by increased interest and fees on loans.
  • Net Interest Income: $76.7 million, indicating a strong performance despite a slight decrease in interest expense.
  • Provision for Credit Losses: $32.2 million, showing a significant decrease compared to the previous year, highlighting improved credit quality.
  • Net Income: $11.0 million, a slight decrease from the previous year, impacted by higher noninterest expenses.
  • Basic Earnings Per Common Share: $0.73, reflecting the company's ability to maintain profitability amidst increased expenses.
  • Diluted Earnings Per Common Share: $0.71, consistent with the company's strategic focus on managing costs and enhancing shareholder value.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: Coastal Financial Corporation operates through three reportable segments: the Community Bank, CCBX, and Treasury & Administration. The Community Bank focuses on providing a wide range of banking products and services to consumers and small to medium-sized businesses in the Puget Sound region. The CCBX segment offers Banking as a Service (BaaS) to digital financial service providers, companies, and brands, allowing them to offer banking services to their customers. As of June 30, 2025, the CCBX segment had 29 partners at varying stages.
  • Geographical Performance: The company operates 14 full-service banking locations, with 12 located in Snohomish County, Washington, where it holds the largest community bank deposit market share. The remaining two branches are in King County and Island County, Washington.
  • Sales Units: The CCBX segment saw significant activity with $2.08 billion in loans transferred to loans held for sale, and $2.04 billion in loans sold during the six months ended June 30, 2025. This reflects the company's strategy to manage loan portfolio size by partner and loan category.
  • Future Outlook: The company continues to focus on expanding its BaaS offerings through the CCBX segment, leveraging its partnerships to drive growth. The management anticipates continued growth in the CCBX segment as more partners are onboarded and existing partners expand their offerings.

SEC Filing: COASTAL FINANCIAL CORP [ CCB ] - 10-Q - Aug. 08, 2025

data.title: Anywhere Real Estate Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Anywhere Real Estate Inc., a leading real estate services company, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational highlights for the three and six months ended June 30, 2025. The company continues to focus on enhancing its service offerings and operational efficiency through strategic initiatives and technological advancements.

Financial Highlights

Net Revenues: $1,682 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $1,669 million for the same period in 2024, reflecting a slight increase driven by higher service revenue.

Net Income: $28 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $30 million for the same period in 2024, indicating a slight decrease due to increased restructuring costs.

Net Income (Loss) for Six Months: $(50) million for the six months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $(71) million for the same period in 2024, showing an improvement in the net loss position.

Basic Earnings (Loss) Per Share: $0.24 for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $0.27 for the same period in 2024, reflecting a decrease in earnings per share.

Diluted Earnings (Loss) Per Share: $0.24 for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $0.27 for the same period in 2024, consistent with the basic earnings per share.

Operating EBITDA: $173 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $172 million for the same period in 2024, indicating stable operating performance.

Total Expenses: $1,650 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $1,631 million for the same period in 2024, reflecting an increase primarily due to higher restructuring costs.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments:

  • The Franchise Group generated $269 million in net revenues for the three months ended June 30, 2025, and $473 million for the six months ended June 30, 2025.
  • The Owned Brokerage Group contributed $1,398 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, and $2,388 million for the six months ended June 30, 2025.
  • The Title Group added $108 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, and $186 million for the six months ended June 30, 2025.

Segment Performance:

  • The Franchise Group's performance was bolstered by its portfolio of well-known franchise brokerage brands and global relocation services.
  • The Owned Brokerage Group, operating under Coldwell Banker®, Corcoran®, and Sotheby’s International Realty® brands, showed strong performance in major U.S. metropolitan areas.
  • The Title Group provided comprehensive title, escrow, and settlement services, supporting residential real estate transactions.

Operational Efficiency: The company launched the Reimagine25 initiative to transform operations, focusing on reimagining branch models, improving technology infrastructure, optimizing leads management, and streamlining finance processes. This initiative aims to simplify, integrate, and digitize operations using advanced technologies.

Future Outlook: The company anticipates further expansion of the Reimagine25 initiative to encompass additional business aspects, aiming to enhance operational efficiency and cost-effectiveness. The focus will remain on leveraging technology to provide better solutions at lower costs.

Equity Method Investments: The company holds various equity method investments totaling $166 million, including a 49.9% interest in the Guaranteed Rate Affinity mortgage origination joint venture and a 22% interest in the Title Insurance Underwriter Joint Venture.

Restructuring Costs: Restructuring charges were $24 million for the six months ended June 30, 2025, primarily related to the Reimagine25 initiative, which includes personnel-related costs, facility-related costs, and other restructuring activities.

Legal and Regulatory Matters: The company is involved in various legal proceedings, including antitrust litigation and a Telephone Consumer Protection Act class action. The Anywhere Settlement, which includes injunctive and monetary relief, aims to resolve claims related to real estate industry practices.

Commitments and Contingencies: The company administers escrow and trust deposits totaling approximately $846 million as of June 30, 2025, which are not assets of the company but represent a contingent liability for the disposition of these deposits.

SEC Filing: Anywhere Real Estate Inc. [ HOUS ] - 10-Q - Aug. 08, 2025

data.title: Firefly Aerospace sputters a day after stellar Nasdaq debut
data.description:
Firefly Aerospace (FLY.O) shares fell 11% in morning trading on Friday, in what was perceived as a normal market swing, but the space tech firm's robust debut highlighted renewed investor appetite for high-growth listings.

After nearly three years of a dryspell in new listings, a resurgence in high-risk sectors such as space, crypto and fintech — fueled by blockbuster entries such as Firefly and Circle — is expected to prompt startups that held back during market turbulence to launch public offerings.

Firefly's shares opened for trading on the Nasdaq at $70 apiece, compared with the initial public offering price of $45. It closed the debut session roughly 34% higher.

"Large pops and drops show an elevated level of short-term money trading around IPOs, be it hedge funds or retail. This has been a feature of the market in recent months," said Samuel Kerr, head of equity capital markets at Mergermarket.

Post-IPO stocks often see sharp swings in the days after listing, driven by factors such as limited share float, profit-taking by early investors and shifts in broader market sentiment.

"The fact the stock is still well above the IPO price, despite a drop in premarket trading today and that should hopefully point to a solid cohort of institutional investors serving as a bed-rock for the stock in its early days of trading," Kerr said.

Firefly had priced its IPO above the marketed range and raised $868.3 million in the year's biggest U.S. space listing, marking a striking comeback for a company that filed for bankruptcy in 2017.

Private space firms have drawn fresh investor interest as they play a growing role in U.S. military and civil programs, aided by NASA's push to contract out lunar missions and the Pentagon's demand for responsive launch capabilities. The sector has also benefited from government spending and commercial satellite demand.

Still, it faces potential challenges from high development costs and long production timelines.

data.title: Centrus Energy Names Tinelli Finance Chief as Harrill Resigns
data.description:
By Colin Kellaher

Centrus Energy's top finance executive, Kevin Harrill, is leaving the supplier of nuclear fuel and services to pursue other opportunities.

Centrus on Friday said it has hired Todd Tinelli to succeed Harrill as senior vice president, chief financial officer and treasurer of the Bethesda, Md., company, effective Aug. 11.

Tinelli, 45 years old, most recently served as chief financial officer of Sprague Resources, Centrus said. He will receive an annual base salary of $425,000 and an annual cash bonus with a target of 80% of his base pay.

Centrus said Harrill, who joined the company in 2021 and has been finance chief since August 2023, will stay on through Aug. 29 to assist with the transition.

Write to Colin Kellaher at colin.kellaher@wsj.com

data.title: TSX inches up as investors eye corporate results, Fed revamp
data.description:
Canada's main stock index inched up on Friday as investors evaluated corporate results and U.S. President Donald Trump's nomination for the open spot in the Federal Reserve.

At 9:31 a.m. ET (1331 GMT), the Toronto Stock Exchange's S&P/TSX composite index was up 0.2% at 27,810.99 points and on track for its largest weekly gain in 11 months.

data.title: Make a Health Care Road Map With These Supplemental Insurance Milestones
data.description:
NORTHAMPTON, MA / ACCESS Newswire / August 8, 2025 / Aflac Incorporated

Originally published on Aflac Newsroom

Starting a new chapter is exciting, but changes can come with challenges. Supplemental insurance coverage offers the opportunity to meet many of those challenges with confidence. From getting married to buying a new home, supplemental insurance helps ensure the special people in these special moments -have added protection.

Here are a few important chapters that should come with some extra assurance.

Turning 26: Happy birthday! Turning 26 means being old enough to vote, rent a car and be removed from a parent's insurance policy. Adulting might seem intimidating, but supplemental insurance can add some security to newfound independence.

Getting married: Congratulations! A wedding is a beautiful event, though a marriage license isn't the only paperwork required. Adding a new spouse to supplemental insurance coverage is an important step in making sure both people have added protection. The new mister or missus must also fill out a continuous coverage verification form.

Planning a family: There's plenty to check off the list when bringing a new baby home - a crib, cute clothes and tons of diapers. But there's an important item that needs to be crossed off before the baby is even on the way, and that's supplemental insurance. Coverage can help provide financial security to protect a growing family.

A new job: Starting a new job means meeting new coworkers and figuring out where to find the breakroom. One way to make the transition easier is to bring along current supplemental insurance coverage. That's where portable policies come into play, which can follow a new employee to their next opportunity.

Traveling: Lost luggage or a delayed flight are common frustrations for the average jetsetter, but it's also important to keep bigger concerns in mind. For example, accident insurance still helps to provide protection while traveling, so qualifying medical expenses incurred away from home are covered.

Life has plenty of plot twists, and Aflac can be there for its customers through each and every one. Visit Aflac.com to learn more and apply for extra protection.

Individual coverage is underwritten by American Family Life Assurance Company of Columbus. In New York, coverage is underwritten by American Family Life Assurance Company of New York.

WWHQ | 1932 Wynnton Road | Columbus, GA 31999

Z2500454

EXP: 06/26

View additional multimedia and more ESG storytelling from Aflac Incorporated on 3blmedia.com.

Contact Info:

Spokesperson: Aflac Incorporated

Website: https://www.3blmedia.com/profiles/aflac-incorporated

Email: info@3blmedia.com
